title: Superconductivity Mediated Long Range Magnetic Coupling
authors: Ming Yan Wang, Yi Liu, Yao Lu
abstract: We study a Rashba superconductor thin film with ferromagnetic insulators (FIs) placed on top of it. We show that the ferromagnetic insulators generate circular super-currents, enabling long-range magnetic interactions (LRMI), decaying in power laws. In the static case, the long-range magnetic interaction can be ferromagnetic, in contrast to previous studies showing that superconductor mediates anti-ferromagnetic interactions decaying exponentially. Surprisingly, we find that in the dynamic case, the LRMI has a different distance dependence. Our results have potential applications in superconducting spintronics.
summary: This paper demonstrates that Rashba superconductors can mediate long-range ferromagnetic interactions between magnetic insulators via power-law decaying supercurrents. It also shows that dynamic magnetization precession leads to even slower spatial decay, offering potential for new mechanisms in superconducting spintronics.
main_problem: Investigating the nature of long-range magnetic coupling between ferromagnetic insulators (FIs) mediated by supercurrents in a 2D Rashba superconductor.
main_result: The study discovers that the mediated interaction is ferromagnetic and follows a power-law decay, specifically $1/x^2$ in the static case and a slower $|x|^{-3/2}$ decay in the dynamic regime.
method: The authors use the London-Maxwell equations, 2D Fourier transforms, and complex analysis (including contour integration and residue theorem) to solve for current distributions and asymptotic behavior.
model_or_system: A 2D Rashba superconductor thin film with ferromagnetic insulator strips placed on top, modeled using the London-Maxwell framework and an effective gauge field induced by spin-orbit coupling.
key_observables: Supercurrent distribution (radial and azimuthal components), induced magnetization, and the spatial decay laws of the magnetic interaction.
important_parameters: Pearl length ($r_0$), Rashba spin-orbit coupling strength, superfluid weight ($D$), magnetization ($\mathbf{m}$), and driving frequency ($\omega$).
main_assumptions: The system is treated in the zero-temperature limit, assumes a 2D/thin-film geometry, and treats the FIs as localized sources or 1D strips.
figures_summary: Fig 1 shows the system schematic; Fig 2 displays streamline plots of supercurrent flow; Fig 3 illustrates the spatial distribution of induced currents for parallel and anti-parallel magnetization.
paper_structure: The paper introduces the physical system, derives the supercurrent distribution in the short-distance limit, analyzes the static coupling regime, extends the analysis to the dynamic (precessing) regime, and provides detailed mathematical proofs in the appendices regarding asymptotic expansions and branch cut integrals.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

figures.caption: FIG. 1: Schematic illustration of the induced transverse current.
FIG. 3: Spatial distribution of the total induced current j(x, y) driven by two FIs located at d1 = 1.0 and d2 = 3.0 with L = 4, W = 2. (a) Parallel state. (b) Anti-parallel state.
FIG. 2: Streamline plot of the supercurrent distribution calculated from the analytical solution in the short distance limit Eq. (7a). The dashed line indicates the FI.
